---
title: A title
subtitle: A subtitle
format: uw-revealjs
author:
  - name: Your Name
    orcid: 0000-0000-0000-0000
    email: alias@email.com
    affiliations: Your Institution
date: last-modified
---

## Example Section

### Example Page

#### This is the heading of one page

$$
\text{logit}\{P(Y_{ij}=1)\}=\gamma_{00}+\gamma_{w}*x^W_{ij}+\gamma_{b}*\bar{x}_{j}+u_{0j}
$$

With the extension defaults, `##` creates a section slide, `###` creates a
separate page within that section, and `####` is the heading for a content
slide.

- first item
    - a sub item

#### Another content slide

Next, we'll briefly review some theme-specific components.

- Note that _all_ of the standard Reveal.js
[features](https://quarto.org/docs/presentations/revealjs/)
can still be used with this theme.

### Theme Utilities

#### Additional theme classes

Special classes for emphasis

- `.alert` class for default emphasis, e.g. [important note]{.alert}.
- `.highlight` class for inline emphasis, e.g. [key idea]{.highlight}.
- `.rounded-box` class for a bordered box around a paragraph or div.
- `.fg` class for custom colour, e.g. [important note]{.fg style="--col: #4b2e83"}.
- `.bg` class for custom background, e.g. [important note]{.bg style="--col: #e8e3d3"}.

Cross-references

- `.button` class provides a Beamer-like button, e.g.
[[Summary]{.button}](#sec-summary)

## Want more?

### Reveal.js Features

#### Standard Reveal.js features still work as usual

This template stays lean so you can adapt it to your own slides quickly.
Standard
[Reveal.js features](https://quarto.org/docs/presentations/revealjs/)
continue to work with the extension.

## Summary

### Wrap-Up

#### A cleaner, more structured presentation theme {#sec-summary}

This extension now uses a more structured reveal.js theme inspired by the
`econometrics-2` slides, while keeping Quarto title metadata like author,
affiliation, and email visible on the title slide. Here are some options to get
you started:

Add the theme to an existing project.

```{.bash}
quarto install extension Zigzag021/quarto-revealjs-uw
```

... or, create a new project using this slide deck as a lean template.

```{.bash}
quarto use template Zigzag021/quarto-revealjs-uw
```
